references.title: Classification and Regression Trees (CART) for Predictive Modeling in Blended Learning
references.journal: International Journal of Intelligent Systems and Applications

extracted.Features: [Messages exchanged, Group wiki content contributions, Files viewed, Quiz efforts]
extracted.Preprocessing Details: IStudent online interactions were extracted from Moodle logs into Excel. VBA macros were used to compute, for each student, the number of files viewed, content contributions, quiz efforts, and messages exchanged. The activity table was then merged with a second file containing student IDs and course grades. CART was applied with four predictors and one binary target.
extracted.Comments: Messages exchanged and collaborative content creation were the two most important predictors. Two main rules emerged: students with messages ≤ 172 were predicted to fail; students with messages > 172 and content contributions ≥ 13 were predicted to succeed. The resulting tree is very small and interpretable.
extracted.Limitations:
Single course in one context. 

Prediction is based on full-semester behavior. 

Very high accuracy may reflect context-specific patterns and may not generalize to other courses or institutions. 

Only four LMS features were used, with no demographic, prior-achievement, or offline learning

annotations.text: Author uses data collected from Moodle to predict student success in a course using CART. Model focuses on detection of students at-risk based on 4 distinct activities: message exchanging, group wiki content creation, course file opening and online quiz taking. Accuracy is close 99%.
